items.title: Tonight in Saline Twp: Data Center Update on agenda
items.dek: The July 8, 2026 regular meeting agenda lists item 9 as 'Data Center Update' (per the agenda PDF). Supervisor's Report also includes 'd. Jupiter' alongside other items like Invenergy and Andelina Farms. No additional staff reports, site plans, or packets linked on the agendas page.
items.findings: Agenda PDF explicitly schedules '9. Data Center Update' as a standalone item | Township maintains a dedicated /data-center-update page with links to Consent Judgment, lawsuit docs, and proposal (per township site) | News coverage attributes the Stargate project (OpenAI/Oracle/Related Digital) to a 250-acre site in Saline Township with ~$16B investment and 1GW capacity (per CNBC, OpenAI announcements) | Groundbreaking for 'The Barn' campus occurred June 1, 2026 (per multiple outlets including DCD and Oracle) | Initial township board vote was 4-1 against the project before later developments (pe

items.title: Democratic Senate candidates clash over AI data center regulation in debate
items.dek: U.S. Rep. Haley Stevens and Abdul El-Sayed offered contrasting views on data centers and AI during last night's Michigan Senate debate, with Stevens emphasizing federal tax fairness and El-Sayed calling for strict community protections and union labor requirements.

items.title: Haley Stevens non-answer on data centers prompts essay reference
items.dek: A post highlighted candidate responses on AI data centers during the Senate race, linking to analysis of Michigan's growing debate.

items.title: John James criticized for data center builder donations amid attacks
items.dek: A Metro Times report noted Republican Senate candidate John James received over $107,000 from Rakolta family and Walbridge executives tied to Michigan data center projects.
